skill.name: skill-share
skill.description: Creates structured Claude skills, packages them for distribution, and shares skill announcements to Slack through Rube.
skill.summary: Create, validate, package, and announce Claude skills for team use.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary prose such as description and designated. The backtick execution finding is also a false positive because lines 59-66 are a fenced example, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill still has medium operational risk because it creates files, packages skills, and posts skill metadata to Slack through Rube.

medium_findings.title: Slack Sharing Can Expose Skill Metadata
medium_findings.description: The skill posts skill information, summaries, metadata, and direct file links to Slack via Rube. This is intended behavior, but users could expose internal project details if they create or share sensitive skills without review.
medium_findings.recommendation: Require explicit channel selection and review skill content before Slack posting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Slack behavior is directly described in the skill instructions. It is legitimate functionality, but metadata and links can reveal internal information.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes and Packaging Require User Control
medium_findings.description: The skill creates directories, generates files, and packages skill assets into zip files. This is necessary for the workflow, but it can create unwanted files or package unintended content if the target path and included assets are not reviewed.
medium_findings.recommendation: Use a dedicated workspace, review generated files, and confirm package contents before distribution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and packaging actions are explicit in the documentation. No malicious intent is shown, but the operations affect local files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm hits on lines 3, 40, 44, 45, 47, and 51 are ordinary documentation text. No cryptographic API, hashing algorithm, cipher configuration, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.recommendation: No cryptography remediation is needed for these documentation lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose only at the reported locations. There is no implementation code or cryptographic primitive to misuse.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Finding Is a Fenced Example
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution occurs inside a Markdown fenced example. It describes expected workflow output and does not execute commands.
low_findings.recommendation: No command-execution remediation is needed for this fenced documentation example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines 59-66 are enclosed in a Markdown code fence and contain numbered prose. There is no shell command, Ruby expression, or user input execution path.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to create Claude skills and announce them for discovery. This skill structures, validates, packages, and shares new skills through Slack.
actual_capabilities: Creates skill directories with SKILL.md and standard supporting folders.
Generates required metadata for new Claude skills.
Validates naming conventions and required skill fields.
Packages skill files into distributable zip archives.
Posts skill announcements and metadata to Slack through Rube.
Finds Slack channels for team skill announcements.
limitations: Requires a connected Slack workspace through Rube.
Requires write access to the selected skill creation directory.
Does not replace human review of generated skill instructions.
May expose skill metadata if users choose the wrong Slack channel.

use_cases.title: Publish Internal AI Workflows
use_cases.description: Create a reusable Claude skill and notify teammates when the package is ready.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Development
use_cases.description: Generate consistent folders, metadata, and validation steps for new team skills.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Discovery
use_cases.description: Share new skill summaries and links in Slack so teams can find approved workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Claude skill named project-notes that helps summarize meeting notes. Include a clear description and standard folders.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Draft Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my new skill for required metadata, naming conventions, and missing structure before I share it.

prompt_templates.title: Package and Announce a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Package the customer-research skill and prepare a Slack announcement with its name, purpose, and file link.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Team Skill Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a skill creation workflow for our team that validates metadata, packages files, and asks me to confirm the Slack channel.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for summarizing product feedback and share it with the team.
output_examples.output: A new product-feedback-summary skill is created with required metadata, standard folders, validation notes, and a prepared Slack announcement.
The support-triage skill is checked for required fields, packaged as a distributable archive, and summarized for review.

output_examples.input: Find the right Slack channel and announce this new skill.
output_examples.output: Available Slack channels are listed for confirmation, then the selected channel receives the approved skill name, summary, and link.

best_practices: Review generated SKILL.md content before packaging or sharing.
Use a dedicated workspace for generated skill files.
Confirm the Slack channel before posting skill links or metadata.
anti_patterns: Do not share skills that contain secrets, credentials, or private customer data.
Do not package broad directories without reviewing included files.
Do not post skill announcements to public channels without approval.

faq.question: Does this skill create complete Claude skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It creates a structured skill directory with SKILL.md and standard supporting folders.

faq.question: Can it share skills outside Slack?
faq.answer: No. The documented sharing integration is Slack through Rube.

faq.question: Does it need filesystem access?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs write access to create folders, generate files, and build packages.

faq.question: Does it run hidden code?
faq.answer: No evidence found in SKILL.md. The file documents workflow steps and requirements.

faq.question: Should generated skills be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. Review instructions, metadata, and packaged files before sharing with a team.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
